title: ferray-autodiff: Rem<T> has dead computation — T::zero() * q is always 0
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

In Rem<T> for DualNumber<T> (line 247), the dual computation is 'self.dual - T::zero() * q' which simplifies to self.dual. The developer's comment says '// rhs is constant, so just self.dual contribution... actually rem with constant' — confused mid-implementation. The result is accidentally correct (d/dx (x % c) = 1 for constant c) but the T::zero() * q dead code obscures intent. Replace with 'dual: self.dual' and add a clarifying comment.
